heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The storm today ranked 9th for the length of the storm when compared to others in our records and it lasted 25.8 hours.  17.83mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 6.6mm per hour.  The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 08 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there has been 1 cold snap, usually there are 3.6 cold snap per year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 0 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (217°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 120.4 mph on 08 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 1.5°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 1.0 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in rainfall (up 1.0 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (down 2.6°C), cloud levels (up 225 m), and outside temperature (down 2.1°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 170.7m higher than yesterday, 213.4m lower than the past week, 225.4m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 492m to 831m and averaged 863.6m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, dry and less humid conditions and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (1.8°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
